Description: Total number of atoms including hydrogens.
Number of non-hydrogen atoms.
Number of atoms of a specific element (e.g. C N O P S F Cl Br I).
Ratio of Carbon atoms to Nitrogen atoms.
Ratio of Carbon atoms to Oxygen atoms.
Ratio of Carbon atoms to Hydrogen atoms.
Ratio of Carbon atoms to Sulfur atoms.
Ratio of Carbon atoms to Halogen atoms (F Cl Br I).
Average molecular mass based on standard atomic weights.
Monoisotopic mass based on the most abundant isotopes.
Total number of bonds in the molecule.
Number of single bonds.
Number of double bonds.
Number of triple bonds.
Number of bonds marked as aromatic.
Number of bonds between atom types X and Y.
Number of smallest independent cycles (SSSR).
Number of 3-membered rings (triangles).
Number of 4-membered rings (squares).
Number of 5-membered rings (pentagons).
Number of 6-membered rings (hexagons).
Number of 7-membered rings (heptagons).
Number of 8-membered rings (octagons).
Number of atoms that are part of at least one ring.
Number of atoms that are not part of any ring.
Ratio of ring atoms to non-ring atoms.
Number of Carbon atoms in rings.
Number of Nitrogen atoms in rings.
Number of Oxygen atoms in rings.
Fraction of total Carbons that are in rings.
Ratio of ring Carbons to ring Nitrogens.
Ratio of ring Carbons to ring Oxygens.
Number of non-carbon non-hydrogen atoms.
Fraction of total atoms that are heteroatoms.
Number of atoms participating in aromatic bonds.
Fraction of total atoms that are aromatic.
Number of freely rotating single bonds between non-terminal heavy atoms (not in rings).
Number of OH and NH groups.
Number of O and N atoms.
Fraction of sp3-hybridized carbons relative to total carbons.
Number of fully aromatic rings.
Number of Lipinski Rule of 5 violations (MW > 500 H-donors > 5 H-acceptors > 10).
An aggregate score based on atoms bonds rings and diversity of elements.
Sum of distances between all pairs of vertices in the molecular graph.
A connectivity index based on vertex degrees.
A distance-based topological index normalized by the cyclomatic number.
Count of specific functional groups detected by substructure search (e.g. Group: Benzene).
